title: Carvana's license to sell vehicles in Illinois suspended - state official
text: Online used-car retailer Carvana Co's license to sell vehicles in Illinois was suspended on May 10 over the mishandling of paperwork related to its vehicle sales, a state official said on Wednesday.

title: Carvana: Liquidity Issues Are Overblown, Secular Growth Narrative Remains Intact

title: AutoNation And Carvana: A Tale Of Two Auto Retailers
text: AutoNation is expanding its store locations across the US, increasing the sourcing of used vehicles and growing post-sales revenues. Carvana took advantage of the Covid pandemic to increase sales and earnings, but has taken a hit from changes in post-pandemic buying patterns.

title: Carvana to Cut 2,500 Workers
text: Online car dealer Carvana will be laying off 12% of its workforce after closing a deal to expand operations, a report from The Wall Street Journal (WSJ) said Tuesday (May 10). According to Ernie Garcia III, CEO, the company had overshot its growth strategy.

title: Carvana layoffs: Stock continues to drop as company discloses plan to shed 12% of workforce
text: Online car dealer Carvana Co. plans to lay off 12% of its workforce after closing a deal to expand operations that forced the company to borrow on onerous terms.

title: Carvana to cut 2,500 staff as it struggles with overcapacity
text: Carvana, a used-car retailer in the United States that raised at least nine figures worth of venture capital while private before going public, announced 2,500 layoffs today.

title: Carvana to lay off about 2,500 employees
text: Online used-car retailer Carvana Co said on Tuesday that it would lay off about 2,500 employees primarily in operational groups.

title: Carvana buys Adesa USA from KAR Global
text: Carvana Co. CVNA, -16.59% said Tuesday it'll pay $2.2 billion for Adesa U.S., the wholesale vehicle auction unit of KAR Auction Services Inc. KAR, -1.89% . The transaction includes 56 Adesa U.S. locations comprising 6.5 million square feet of buildings and 4,000-plus acres.

title: Carvana stock dips after Wells Fargo downgrade cites 'sharply changing macro'
text: Shares of Carvana Co. CVNA, +4.33% were off 3% in premarket trading Tuesday after Wells Fargo analyst Zachary Fadem downgraded the stock to equal-weight from overweight in the wake of an "ugly" earnings report in late April and new updates on financing for the company's pending acquisition of Adesa's U.S. physical auction business. "While we believe CVNA is still on a long-term path to becoming the largest, most profitable used car retailer in this country, the near-term road is proving much less linear than we initially thought," he wrote in a note to clients.
